regimeSub: Risk-on melt-up on US-Iran de-escalation, but sticky 4.2% CPI and a Warsh Fed flirting with hikes keep the trap door open.
themes: US-Iran ceasefire caps oil near $73 -> tail risk drains, equities rip to record highs
CPI re-accelerates to 4.2% on energy -> Fed dots flip hawkish, September hike back on table
Warsh Fed overhaul + hike risk -> front-end stays anchored, real rates still negative
AI/energy capex supercycle -> mega-cap tech leads Dow above 52k despite tighter policy

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed behind the curve, hike bias building.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp and positively sloped; bear-flattening risk if Warsh delivers the threatened hike.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; risk appetite robust on ceasefire and melt-up, no stress priced.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65 — complacent; Iran tail removed but ignores September hike risk.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73 after US-Iran deal halts attacks; supply-surge narrative vs sticky energy CPI.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: ~$3997 near records; hedging negative real rates and Fed credibility under Warsh.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1546 weak won despite KOSPI at 8643; carry and tech inflows offset soft dollar.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/energy capex boom driving mega-cap tech leadership; key engine of the melt-up.

headline: Markets are melting up to records as the US-Iran ceasefire crushes the oil tail, but 4.2% inflation and a hawkish Warsh Fed threatening a September hike leave reflation running hot into a policy trap.
